data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-10T01:36:25.540Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating this .NET Framework code to .NET 8 and need to ensure all Thread.Abort
usage is addressed. Check this code and provide your Thread.Abort migration guidance.

```csharp
public class SimpleWorker
{
    public void RunBatch(List<WorkItem> items)
    {
        var threads = new List<Thread>();

        foreach (var item in items)
        {
            var t = new Thread(() => Process(item));
            t.Start();
            threads.Add(t);
        }

        foreach (var t in threads)
            t.Join();
    }

    private void Process(WorkItem item)
    {
        // simulate work
        Thread.Sleep(100);
        item.Result = ComputeResult(item.Input);
    }
}
```

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about migrating .NET Framework code to .NET 8, specifically around `Thread.Abort`. Let me analyze the code they provided.

Looking at the code, it doesn't actually use `Thread.Abort` — it uses `Thread` with `Start`/`Join` patterns. The user wants migration guidance, so I should point out that while this code doesn't call `Thread.Abort` directly, the broader concern is that `Thread.Abort` throws `ThreadAbortException` in .NET Framework but throws `PlatformNotSupportedException` in .NET Core/.NET 5+. 

I should provide practical guidance on modernizing this code to use `Task`-based patterns with `CancellationToken` for cooperative cancellation, which is the recommended replacement.
